title: Twelfth Night: Or, King And Queen.
content:
Now, now the mirth comes
    With the cake full of plums,
    Where bean's the king of the sport here;
    Beside we must know,
    The pea also
    Must revel, as queen, in the court here.

    Begin then to choose,
    This night as ye use,
    Who shall for the present delight here,
    Be a king by the lot,
    And who shall not
    Be Twelfth-day queen for the night here.

    Which known, let us make
    Joy-sops with the cake;
    And let not a man then be seen here,
    Who unurg'd will not drink
    To the base from the brink
    A health to the king and the queen here.

    Next crown the bowl full
    With gentle lamb's wool:
    Add sugar, nutmeg, and ginger,
    With store of ale too;
    And thus ye must do
    To make the wassail a swinger.

    Give then to the king
    And queen wassailing:
    And though with ale ye be whet here,
    Yet part ye from hence,
    As free from offence
    As when ye innocent met here.
meaning:
"Twelfth Night: Or, King And Queen." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
